signals.reasoning: Cover the live MON short immediately. The trade remains deeply underwater, liquidation distance is still uncomfortably tight, and with veto active there is even less reason to keep defending a broken position.
BTC is steady, but the veto window means there is no reason to open fresh risk in a benchmark coin without a much stronger edge.
ETH remains mixed and does not offer enough downside clarity to justify a new short under the current event brake.
SOL still has some constructive structure, but the event veto overrides the urge to put on a fresh long.
CHILLGUY still looks stretched, but the veto regime means this is watch-only rather than a fresh fade entry.
ZEREBRO has momentum but poor trade quality, so it stays on the sidelines.
GRASS remains a noisy momentum name without enough edge or liquidity quality to act on under veto conditions.
TAO is still the cleanest bearish name on the rest of the sheet because price remains weak and the board keeps a downside bias, though conviction is capped by the broader veto state.
ENA has momentum but not enough structural edge to overcome the event brake.
TNSR still has squeeze-long characteristics with negative funding, but veto should keep it in watch mode rather than buy mode for now.
DASH is extended and not offering a clean enough setup to justify action while the event veto is active.
